feat: add Stop button in Playground to terminate idle workers

- Add RunPod `update_endpoint_idle_timeout` GraphQL mutation
- Add `POST /v1/models/{slug}/terminate` endpoint (sets idleTimeout=5s)
- Update `/warm` to restore idleTimeout=30s on next wake-up
- Add Stop button in Playground UI (visible when worker is ready/warming_up)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch back to input seeking, remove -copyts flag

Output seeking gets progressively slower for later segments since
ffmpeg reads from file start each time. Input seeking is fast but
was producing empty output — likely caused by -copyts confusing
timestamp handling. Removing -copyts lets ffmpeg reset timestamps
per segment which should work with HLS EXTINF-based timing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace modal bottom sheet with inline preview on map screen

showModalBottomSheet created scrim barriers that stacked when tapping
pins on YandexMap (platform view). Each tap leaked through the barrier,
opening new sheets and progressively darkening the map.

Changes:
- Replace showModalBottomSheet with inline Positioned preview in Stack
- Add close button (X) and swipe-down-to-dismiss gesture
- Add selected marker state (yellow highlight) with zIndex priority
- Pre-generate selected marker variant in MapMarkerGenerator

commit.message:
expr: extend #[sqlfunc] to variadic functions and convert all eager variadics (#34985)

Add the `EagerVariadicFunc` trait with a blanket `LazyVariadicFunc`
implementation, and extend the `#[sqlfunc]` proc macro to generate
`EagerVariadicFunc` implementations for variadic functions.
Convert all 30 eager variadic functions from hand-written dispatch to
`#[sqlfunc]`-derived implementations, then extend `derive_variadic!` to
generate the full `VariadicFunc` dispatch.

---------

Signed-off-by: Moritz Hoffmann <mh@materialize.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: remove mock data, add connection error page, notification edit mode, and history summary redesign

- Remove MockDataService entirely and all FetchOrFallbackAsync patterns
- Add ConnectionErrorPage with retry button for API unreachable scenarios
- Replace notification swipe gestures with edit mode (select, bulk delete, bulk mark read)
- Redesign history summary boxes with gradient backgrounds and icons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: HLD agent must not file LLD issues; fix test hangs in design tests (#275)

- Remove LLD issue filing from HLD agent skill and issue body.
  The HLD agent's only job is to write HLD.md. The orchestrator's Phase 2
  self-heal already parses `### Module:` headings from the merged HLD and
  creates LLD issues via `_file_design_issue_if_missing` (bead-based dedup).
  Having the agent file issues via gh CLI causes duplicates because GitHub's
  eventual consistency means newly created issues aren't indexed immediately.

- Fix _DESIGN_PATCHES["repo_root"] to patch _ensure_worktree_repo (the
  function actually called) instead of _get_repo_root (never called in
  _run_design_worker). Update return_value to ("/repo", None) tuple.

- Add _DESIGN_PATCHES["parse_modules"] → _parse_modules_from_hld and patch
  it in all tests that reach Phase 2, eliminating real gh api network calls.

- Add missing list_research patches to tests that enter Gate 1 without
  patching _list_all_open_issues_by_label (which makes real gh calls).

- Add classify_blocking patch to test_proceeds_when_only_deferred_research:
  _classify_blocking_issues is now bead-only (no [DEFERRED] body tag check),
  so without store the issue was always blocking → infinite Gate 1 loop.

Co-authored-by: Design Worker <agent@bread-wood.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
add enable_export_to_zip and enable_upload_to_dashboard flags

Both flags are read from .env (true/false strings).
Defaults: export=true, upload=false.

The scheduler logs the flag values each cycle and skips the
corresponding step when disabled. Export is skipped if model.zip
already exists; upload is skipped if dashboard_uploaded.txt is present.

feat(icons): importing sax iconography (#271)

feat(icons): migrate to design system iconography

Replace all incorrectly imported sax icons with correct SVGs from the
design source. Renames TumbDown/TumbUp to ThumbDown/ThumbUp, removes
duplicate FireIcon in favour of FlameIcon, and strips no-op
useId/mask/clipPath wrappers from icons where they served no purpose.

Each icon uses currentColor for theming, forwardRef, className/size
overrides via cn(), and aria-hidden for accessibility. Adds full test
coverage for all icons.

- riscaldamento: Termostato digitale 230V (Siemens RDH10RF)
              + Resistenza cartuccia industriale 1kW (Vulcanic CR-1000-230)
- rete: Switch PoE 8p Gigabit (TP-Link TL-SG1008P)
      + Access Point WiFi 6 PoE (Ubiquiti U6-Lite)
- domotica: Attuatore KNX 4ch 16A (Schneider MTN6730-0004)
          + Gateway Modbus RTU/TCP (Moxa MGate MB3170)
- idraulica: Pressostato digitale 0-10bar (Danfoss MBS 3000)
           + Pompa circolatore monofase (Grundfos UPS 25-60)

Totale: 24 componenti (era 15), ingesto DB: 9 ins + 13 guasti

Add Wave Function Collapse and Percolation maze algorithms

commit.message:
Auto mode: call-chain leak resolution, call-graph caller stubbing, and final baseline run

- Parse full [checkct:result] call stacks to resolve leaks to the deepest
  BN function with an available stub (falling back to callers)
- Infer call graph from [sse:debug] function transitions using per-pair
  first-transition direction heuristic
- Walk up the call graph for dominant functions (>5% trace) without stubs
  to find the closest ancestor with a stub (e.g. bn_mul_mont → bn_mul_mont_fixed_top)
- After convergence, run a final baseline analysis (no BN, no stubs) with
  timeout = per_timeout × total_iterations
- Document the full auto mode algorithm in README.md

Fix seeking: use output seeking (-ss after -i) for WORKERFS compat

Input seeking (-ss before -i) with WORKERFS produces empty segments
for non-zero positions. Output seeking works correctly since ffmpeg
parses the container metadata first, then seeks using it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Initial implementation of Bittensor Miner Deployer

A full-stack web application for simulating Bittensor miner deployments
on GPU subnets, built as a demo for Polaris Cloud.

Backend (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + PostgreSQL):
- REST API with 15+ endpoints for subnets, deployments, and config
- Database models: subnets, deployments, deployment_logs, user_configs
- Excel parser for importing subnet data from spreadsheets
- Simulation engine with realistic deployment flow:
  - Provisioning phase (2-5s mock VM allocation)
  - Installation phase (15-20s with realistic log messages)
  - Running phase with periodic metrics updates
- Async background tasks for deployment simulation

Frontend (React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS):
- 8 pages: Dashboard, Subnets, Deployments, Deploy Wizard, Settings, Import
- Zustand for state management
- Real-time deployment logs viewer
- Status polling for live updates
- Responsive UI with Tailwind CSS

Features:
- Import GPU subnet data from Excel files
- Browse and filter subnets by GPU type, VRAM, category
- Deploy miners with simulated lifecycle
- Monitor deployments with live logs and metrics
- Start/stop/delete deployment controls

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
